name: cd-zoneless
title: Change-detection signal-emission timing under zoneless
description: Pattern for understanding when signal emissions trigger change detection in zoneless Angular, and how to compose computed/effect to avoid common pitfalls. Pure pattern entry — no component source.

cena.whenToUse: Whenever authoring a component or service that uses signals (input(), model(), signal(), computed(), effect()) under provideZonelessChangeDetection().
principles: Signal reads inside templates are auto-tracked; the framework re-renders the component when a tracked signal changes
computed() is lazy + memoized; it does NOT re-evaluate until a consumer reads it AND a dependency changed since last read
effect() runs after the consumer's microtask boundary; do NOT mutate a signal inside an effect that also reads it (creates a loop) — guard with untracked() if needed
input() is read-only inside the component; output() emits via EventEmitter; model() is two-way (consumer can read + set)
linkedSignal seeds from another signal and stays linked; useful for 'derive from input but allow local override' patterns (see input.directive.ts — size = linkedSignal(() => zSize()))
gotchas: DOM imperative mutations do NOT trigger change detection under zoneless — use signals + computed classes (see button.component.ts host bindings for the canonical pattern)
afterNextRender runs after the next render, after the microtask queue drains — use for DOM measurement and observer setup (see button.component.ts MutationObserver setup for the canonical pattern)
Avoid mixing signals with RxJS observables in the same flow; pick one for clarity. When bridging is required, use toSignal() at the boundary

preferenceLedgerRows.name: Zoneless change detection
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: this entire pattern is the zoneless contract

references: In-catalog references: button.component.ts (afterNextRender + MutationObserver + computed host bindings); input.directive.ts (linkedSignal + effect + signal-based ControlValueAccessor)
